words.definition: able to be believed or trusted
words.example: The witness gave a credible account of what happened.
The athlete's speed was incredible to witness.

words.definition: too willing to believe things without enough evidence
words.example: A credulous person is easily fooled by scams.

words.definition: unwilling or unable to believe something
words.example: She was incredulous when she heard the surprising news.

words.definition: qualifications or achievements that make someone trusted
words.example: The doctor's credentials were displayed on the wall.
The volcano began to rupt without warning.

words.definition: to break into a conversation or activity
words.example: Please do not interrupt while someone is speaking.
The meeting came to an abrupt end.

words.definition: to break apart the normal flow of something
words.example: The storm disrupted train services across the city.

words.definition: to break out suddenly with force
words.example: The crowd erupted in applause after the speech.

words.definition: to say something aloud for someone else to write down
words.example: The manager would dictate letters to her assistant.

words.definition: to say what will happen before it occurs
words.example: Experts predict rain for the weekend.

words.definition: the official decision spoken in a court of law
words.example: The jury delivered its verdict after two days.

words.definition: to say the opposite of what someone else said
words.example: The new evidence seemed to contradict his statement.

words.definition: the way words are chosen and spoken
words.example: Her diction was clear and easy to understand.
